None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate a timeout_ms value.\n\n    Args:\n        value: The timeout value to validate\n        source_name: Name of the source for error messages\n\n    Returns:\n        The validated timeout as int in seconds, or None if not specified\n\n    Raises:\n        FetchConfigValidationError: If the timeout value is invalid\n    \"\"\"\n    if value is None:\n        return None\n\n    # Must be an integer or float that can be converted to int\n    if not isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n        raise FetchConfigValidationError([\n            f\"{source_name}: _load_fetch_config() -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Load fetch configuration from YAML file.\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with source timeouts. Empty dict if file not found or invalid.\n\n    Raises:\n        FetchConfigValidationError: If config schema is invalid\n    \"\"\"\n    global _fetch_config_cache, _fetch_config_mtime\n\n    # Check if we need to reload (file modified or not loaded yet)\n    try:\n        current_mtime = FETCH_CONFIG_PATH.stat().st_mtime\n        if _fetch_config_cache is not None and current_mtime == _fetch_config_mtime:\n            return _fetch_config_cache\n    except OSError:\n        # File doesn't exist or can't be accessed\n        return {}\n\n    # Load the YAML file\n    try:\n        config = yaml.safe_load(FETCH_CONFIG_PATH.read_text()) or {}\n    except OSError:\n        return {}  # File doesn't exist\n\n    # Validate the config structure\n    errors = []\n\n    if \"sources\" in config:\n        if not isinstance(config[\"sources\"], dict):\n            errors.append(\"'sources' must be a dictionary\")\n        else:\n            # Validate each source timeout\n            for source_name, timeout_config in config[\"sources\"].items():\n                if not isinstance(timeout_config, dict):\n                    errors.append(f\"sources.{source_name}: must be a dictionary, got {type(timeout_config).__name__}\")\n                    continue\n\n                if \"timeout_ms\" in timeout_config:\n                    try:\n                        _validate_timeout_ms(timeout_config[\"timeout_ms\"], f\"sources.{source_name}\")\n                    except FetchConfigValidationError as e:\n                        errors.extend(e.errors)\n\n    if errors:\n        raise FetchConfigValidationError(errors)\n\n    # Cache the validated config\n    _fetch_config_cache = config\n    _fetch_config_mtime = current_mtime\n\n    return config\n\n\ndef get_source_timeout_ms(source: FetchSource, project_slug: str | None = None) -> int | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Get the configured timeout (in seconds) for a fetch source.\n\n    Checks the config file for timeout_ms value and converts to seconds.\n    Priority order:\n    1. Project-specific override (if project_slug provided)\n    2. Global source-specific timeout\n    3. None (use default from spec)\n\n    Args:\n        source: The fetch source to get timeout for\n        project_slug: Optional project slug for project-specific overrides\n\n    Returns:\n        Timeout in seconds, or None if not configured\n\n    Raises:\n        FetchConfigValidationError: If config is invalid (on hot-reload)\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        config = _load_fetch_config()\n    except FetchConfigValidationError:\n        # On validation error, return None (use default) but log would be useful\n        return None\n\n    source_key = source.value  # Use enum value as key\n\n    # Check for project-specific override FIRST (if provided)\n    if project_slug:\n        project_timeouts = config.get(\"project_timeouts\", {})\n        if project_slug in project_timeouts:\n            project_config = project_timeouts[project_slug]\n            if isinstance(project_config, dict) and source_key in project_config:\n                source_override = project_config[source_key]\n                if isinstance(source_override, dict) and \"timeout_ms\" in source_override:\n                    timeout_ms = source_override[\"timeout_ms\"]\n                    if timeout_ms is not None:\n                        return int(timeout_ms) / 1000\n\n    # Check for global source-specific timeout\n    sources_config = config.get(\"sources\", {})\n    if source_key in sources_config:\n        source_config = sources_config[source_key]\n        if \"timeout_ms\" in source_config:\n            timeout_ms = source_config[\"timeout_ms\"]\n            if timeout_ms is not None:\n                # Convert milliseconds to seconds\n                return int(timeout_ms) / 1000\n\n    # No timeout configured\n    return None\n\n\ndef get_effective_timeout(spec: FetchCommandSpec, project_slug: str | None = None) -> int | float:\n    \"\"\"\n    Get the effective timeout for a fetch command spec.\n\n    Priority order:\n    1. ----------------------------------------------------\n40\tclass IntentCache:\n41\t    \"\"\"\n42\t    In-memory cache store for intent classifications with TTL support.\n43\t\n44\t    Storage format: {cache_key -> (intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp)}\n45\t    - cache_key: SHA256 hash of utterance + optional context\n46\t    - intent_mapping: list[IntentClassification] to return on cache hit\n47\t    - expiry_timestamp: Unix timestamp when entry expires (now + ttl_seconds)\n48\t\n49\t    Automatic cleanup:\n50\t    - Expired entries are removed on get() when cache size > 1000\n51\t    - Oldest entry is evicted when cache reaches capacity\n52\t\n53\t    Thread-safety: Not thread-safe - assumes single-threaded async execution.\n54\t    \"\"\"\n55\t\n56\t    def __init__(self, ttl_seconds: int = 300, max_size: int = 1000):\n57\t        \"\"\"\n58\t        Initialize the cache.\n59\t\n60\t        Args:\n61\t            ttl_seconds: Time-to-live for cache entries in seconds (default: 300 = 5 minutes)\n62\t            max_size: Maximum number of entries before eviction (default: 1000)\n63\t        \"\"\"\n64\t        self.ttl_seconds = ttl_seconds\n65\t        self.max_size = max_size\n66\t        self._cache: dict[str, tuple[list, float]] = {}\n67\t        self._cache_hits = 0\n68\t        self._cache_misses = 0\n69\t        self._stats_log_interval = 50  # Log stats every N operations\n70\t\n71\t    def get(self, key: str) -> list | None:\n72\t        \"\"\"\n73\t        Retrieve cached intent mapping if exists and not expired.\n74\t\n75\t        Args:\n76\t            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n77\t\n78\t        Returns:\n79\t            Cached intent mapping if exists and not expired, None otherwise\n80\t        \"\"\"\n81\t        # Automatic cleanup: if cache size > 1000, remove all expired entries first\n82\t        # This prevents memory leaks from accumulating expired entries\n83\t        if len(self._cache) > 1000:\n84\t            removed = self._cache:\n89\t            intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp = self._cache[key]\n90\t\n91\t            # Check if entry has expired\n92\t            if time.time() < expiry_timestamp:\n93\t                self._cache_hits += 1\n94\t                age = time.time() - (expiry_timestamp - self.ttl_seconds)\n95\t                logger.debug(f\"Cache HIT for key {key[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n96\t                return intent_mapping\n97\t            else:\n98\t                # Remove expired entry\n99\t                del self._cache[key]\n100\t                logger.debug(f\"Cache EXPIRED for key {key[:8]}\")\n101\t\n102\t        # Cache miss or expired\n103\t        self._cache_misses += 1\n104\t        return None\n105\t\n106\t    def set(self, key: str, value: list) -> None:\n107\t        \"\"\"\n108\t        Store intent mapping with expiry timestamp.\n109\t\n110\t        Args:\n111\t            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n112\t            value: Intent mapping to cache Handle None/empty context gracefully - treat as empty string\n325\t        context = intent_type_context or \"\"\n326\t\n327\t        # Combine utterance and context with a delimiter to avoid collisions\n328\t        # e.g., \"test\" + \"status\" vs \"teststatus\" + \"\"\n329\t        combined = f\"{utterance}|{context}\"\n330\t\n331\t        # Generate SHA256 hash and return hex digest (32 characters)\n332\t        return hashlib.sha256(combined.encode()).hexdigest()\n333\t\n334\t    def _get_cached_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\n335\t        \"\"\"Check cache for existing classification.\"\"\"\n336\t        cache_key = self.generate_cache_key(utterance, session_id)\n337\t        return self._cache.get(cache_key)\n338\t\n339\t    def _cache_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\n340\t        \"\"\"Cache classification result.\"\"\"\n341\t        cache_key = self.generate_cache_key(utterance, session_id)\n342\t        self._cache.set(cache_key, classifications)\n343\t\n344\t    def _get_cache_stats_string(self) -> str:\n345\t        \"\"\"\n346\t        Get formatted cache statistics string for logging.\n347\t\n348\t        Returns:\n349\t            Formatted string with cache hit rate and entries count\n350\t        \"\"\"\n351\t        stats = self._cache.get_stats()\n352\t        return f\"cache_hit_rate={stats['hit_rate']:.2f}% entries={stats['size']}\"\n353\t\n354\t    def _log_cache_stats_if_needed(self) -> None:\n355\t        \"\"\"Log cache hit rate statistics periodically.\"\"\"\n356\t        if self._cache.should_log_stats():\n357\t            stats = self._cache.get_stats()\n358\t            logger.info(\n359\t                f\"Router cache statistics: \"\n360\t                f\"hits={stats['hits']} misses={stats['misses']} \"\n361\t                f\"hit_rate={stats['hit_rate']:.1f}% \"\n362\t                f\"total_requests={stats['hits'] + stats['misses']} \"\n363\t                f\"cache_size={stats['size']}\"\n364\t            )\n365\t\n366\t    def _clear_cache(self) -> None:\n367\t        \"\"\"Clear the router cache. Used primarily for testing and diagnostics.\"\"\"\n368\t        self._cache.clear()\n369\t\n370\t    async def _get_store(self):\n371\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n372\t        if self.store is None:\n373\t            self.store = get_store()\n374\t        return self.store\n375\t\n376\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n377\t        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n378\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n379\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n380\t        return self._reload_manager\n381\t\n382\t    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n383\t        \"\"\"\n384\t        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n385\t\n386\t        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n387\t        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n388\t        \"\"\"\n389\t        try:\n390\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n391\t        except Exception as e:\n392\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n393\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n394\t\n395\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n396\t        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n397\t\n398\t        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n399\t        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n400\t        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n401\t        \"\"\"\n402\t        return self._load_router_prompt()\n403\t\n404\t    async def classify_utterance(\n405\t        self,\n406\t        utterance: str,\n407\t        session_id: str,\n408\t        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n409\t    ) -> tuple[list[IntentClassification], dict]:\n410\t        \"\"\"\n411\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n412\t\n413\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n414\t\n415\t        Args:\n416\t            utterance: The user utterance\n417\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n418\t            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n419\t\n420\t        Returns:\n421\t            Tuple of (classifications list, timing_breakdown dict)\n422\t\n423\t        Raises:\n424\t            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n425\t            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n426\t            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n427\t            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n428\t        \"\"\"\n429\t        # OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\n430\t        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\n431\t        if cached_result is not None:\n432\t            # Log cache statistics periodically\n433\t            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n434\t\n435\t            # Log cache hit with cache statistics in timing breakdown format\n436\t            cache_stats = self._get_cache_stats_string()\n437\t            logger.info(\n438\t                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n439\t                f\"cached=True \"\n440\t                f\"intents={len(cached_result)} \"\n441\t                f\"{cache_stats}\"\n442\t            )\n443\t\n444\t            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n445\t            empty_breakdown = {\n446\t                \"cached\": True,\n447\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n448\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n449\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n450\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n451\t                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n452\t                \"process_ms\": 0,\n453\t                \"total_ms\": 0,\n454\t                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n455\t            }\n456\t            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n457\t\n458\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n459\t        retry_count = 0\n460\t        raw_response = None\n461\t\n462\t        try:\n463\t            # Measure total classification time\n464\t            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n465\t\n466\t            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n467\t            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n468\t\n469\t            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n470\t            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n471\t            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n472\t\n473\t            # Build user message\n474\t            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n475\t\n476\t            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n477\t\n478\t            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n479\t            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n480\t            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n481\t\n482\t            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n483\t\n484\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n485\t            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n486\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n487\t            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n488\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n489\t                user_message=user_message,\n490\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n491\t                max_tokens=96,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 128 to 96 - intent JSON is typically 40-80 tokens (single: ~40-60, multi: ~80-100)\n492\t                temperature=0.0,\n493\t                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n494\t            )\n495\t            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n496\t\n497\t            # Extract content and timing from response\n498\t            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n499\t            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n500\t            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n501\t            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n502\t\n503\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n504\t            raw_response = response\n505\t\n506\t            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\n507\t            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\n508\t            calculated_inference_ms = None\n509\t            if timing_network_ms is not None:\n510\t                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\n511\t\n512\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\n513\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n514\t            try:\n515\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n516\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n517\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n518\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n519\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n520\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\n521\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n522\t                raise e\n523\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n524\t\n525\t            # Measure classification processing time\n526\t            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n527\t            classifications = []\n528\t\n529\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n530\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n531\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n532\t                try:\n533\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n534\t                except ValueError:\n535\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n536\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n537\t\n538\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n539\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n540\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n541\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n542\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n543\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n544\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n545\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n546\t                )\n547\t                classifications.append(classification)\n548\t            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n549\t\n550\t            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n551\t\n552\t            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n553\t            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n554\t            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n555\t            calculated_inference_str = f\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n556\t            cache_stats = self._get_cache_stats_string()\n557\t            logger.info(\n558\t                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n559\t                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n560\t                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n561\t                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n562\t                f\"proxy_inference_ms={calculated_inference_str} \"\n563\t                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n564\t                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n565\t                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n566\t                f\"intents={len(classifications)} \"\n567\t                f\"{cache_stats}\"\n568\t            )\n569\t\n570\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n571\t\n572\t            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n573\t            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n574\t            timing_breakdown = {\n575\t                \"cached\": False,  # Cache miss - result from fresh ZAI proxy call\n576\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n577\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n578\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n579\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\n580\t                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n581\t                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n582\t                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n583\t                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n584\t            }\n585\t\n586\t            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n587\t            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n588\t\n589\t            # Log cache statistics periodically (including this cache miss)\n590\t            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n591\t\n592\t            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n593\t\n594\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n595\t            \"\"\"\n596\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n597\t\n598\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n599\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n600\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n601\t            \"\"\"\n602\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n603\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n604\t\n605\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n606\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n607\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n608\t                retry_count += 1\n609\t                try:\n610\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n611\t                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n612\t                        utterance=utterance,\n613\t                        session_id=session_id,\n614\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n615\t                    )\n616\t                    return classifications, _\n617\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n618\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n619\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n620\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n621\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n622\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n623\t                    ) from retry_e\n624\t\n625\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n626\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n627\t                parse_error=str(e),\n628\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n629\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n630\t            ) from e\n631\t\n632\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n633\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n634\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n635\t\n636\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n637\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n638\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n639\t\n640\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n641\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n642\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n643\t\n644\t        except Exception as e:\n645\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n646\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n647\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n648\t\n649\t    async def route_utterance(\n650\t        self,\n651\t        utterance: str,\n652\t        utterance_id: str,\n653\t        session_id: str,\n654\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n655\t        \"\"\"\n656\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n657\t\n658\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n659\t\n660\t        Args:\n661\t            utterance: The user utterance\n662\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n663\t            session_id: Session ID\n664\t\n665\t        Returns:\n666\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n667\t\n668\t        Raises:\n669\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n670\t        \"\"\"\n671\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n672\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n673\t\n674\t        try:\n675\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n676\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n677\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n678\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n679\t            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n680\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n681\t\n682\t            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n683\t            try:\n684\t                store = await self._get_store()\n685\t                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n686\t                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n687\t            except Exception as e:\n688\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n689\t                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n690\t\n691\t            routed_intents = []\n692\t            for classification in classifications:\n693\t                # Create intent ID\n694\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n695\t\n696\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n697\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n698\t                    classification=classification,\n699\t                    session_id=session_id,\n700\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n701\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n702\t                )\n703\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n704\t\n705\t            return routed_intents\n706\t\n707\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n708\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n709\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n710\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n711\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n712\t                utterance=utterance,\n713\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n714\t                session_id=session_id,\n715\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n716\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n717\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n718\t            )\n719\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n720\t\n721\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n722\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n723\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n724\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n725\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n726\t                utterance=utterance,\n727\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n728\t                session_id=session_id,\n729\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n730\t            )\n731\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n732\t\n733\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n734\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n735\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n736\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n737\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n738\t                utterance=utterance,\n739\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n740\t                session_id=session_id,\n741\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n742\t            )\n743\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n744\t\n745\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n746\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n747\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n748\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n749\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n750\t                utterance=utterance,\n751\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n752\t                session_id=session_id,\n753\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n754\t            )\n755\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n756\t\n757\t    async def process_intent(\n758\t        self,\n759\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n760\t    ) -> dict:\n761\t        \"\"\"\n762\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n763\t\n764\t        Args:\n765\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n766\t\n767\t        Returns:\n768\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n769\t        \"\"\"\n770\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n771\t\n772\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n773\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n774\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n775\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n776\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n777\t        # the dispatch itself.\n778\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n779\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n780\t\n781\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n782\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n783\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n784\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n785\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n786\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n787\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n788\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n789\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n790\t            )\n791\t\n792\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n793\t            store = await self._get_store()\n794\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n795\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n796\t                \"resolved\",\n797\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n798\t            )\n799\t\n800\t            return {\n801\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n802\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n803\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n804\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n805\t            }\n806\t\n807\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n808\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n809\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n810\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n811\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n812\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n813\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n814\t            )\n815\t\n816\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n817\t            store = await self._get_store()\n818\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n819\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n820\t                \"resolved\",\n821\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n822\t            )\n823\t\n824\t            return {\n825\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n826\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n827\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n828\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n829\t            }\n830\t\n831\t        try:\n832\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n833\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n834\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n835\t            else:\n836\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n837\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n838\t        except Exception:\n839\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n840\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n841\t            raise\n842\t\n843\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n844\t        return result\n845\t\n846\t    async def _persist_timings(\n847\t        self,\n848\t        intent_id: str,\n849\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n850\t    ) -> None:\n851\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n852\t        try:\n853\t            store = await self._get_store()\n854\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n855\t        except Exception as e:\n856\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n857\t\n858\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n859\t        self,\n860\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n861\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n862\t    ) -> dict:\n863\t        \"\"\"\n864\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n865\t\n866\t        Args:\n867\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n868\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n869\t                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n870\t\n871\t        Returns:\n872\t            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n873\t        \"\"\"\n874\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n875\t\n876\t        logger.info(\n877\t            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n878\t            f\"(type: support.\n\n    Storage format: {cache_key -> (intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp)}\n    - cache_key: SHA256 hash of utterance + optional context\n    - intent_mapping: list[IntentClassification] to return on cache hit\n    - expiry_timestamp: Unix timestamp when entry expires (now + ttl_seconds)\n\n    Automatic cleanup:\n    - Expired entries are removed on get() when cache size > 1000\n    - Oldest entry is evicted when cache reaches capacity\n\n    Thread-safety: Not thread-safe - assumes single-threaded async execution.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, ttl_seconds: int = 300, max_size: int = 1000):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize the cache.\n\n        Args:\n            ttl_seconds: Time-to-live for cache entries in seconds (default: 300 = 5 minutes)\n            max_size: Maximum number of entries before eviction (default: 1000)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.ttl_seconds = ttl_seconds\n        self.max_size = max_size\n        self._cache: dict[str, tuple[list, float]] = {}\n        self._cache_hits = 0\n        self._cache_misses = 0\n        self._stats_log_interval = 50  # Log stats every N operations\n\n    def get(self, key: str) -> list | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Retrieve cached intent mapping if exists and not expired.\n\n        Args:\n            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n\n        Returns:\n            Cached intent mapping if exists and not expired, None otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        # Automatic cleanup: if cache size > 1000, remove all expired entries first\n        # This prevents memory leaks from accumulating expired entries\n        if len(self._cache) > 1000:\n            removed = self._cleanup_expired()\n            if removed > 0:\n                logger.debug(f\"Auto-cleanup removed {removed} expired entries (size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n\n        if key in self._cache:\n            intent_mapping, expiry_timestamp = self._cache[key]\n\n            # Check if entry has expired\n            if time.time() < expiry_timestamp:\n                self._cache_hits += 1\n                age = time.time() - (expiry_timestamp - self.ttl_seconds)\n                logger.debug(f\"Cache HIT for key {key[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n                return intent_mapping\n            else:\n                # Remove expired entry\n                del self._cache[key]\n                logger.debug(f\"Cache EXPIRED for key {key[:8]}\")\n\n        # Cache miss or expired\n        self._cache_misses += 1\n        return None\n\n    def set(self, key: str, value: list) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Store intent mapping with expiry timestamp.\n\n        Args:\n            key: Cache key (SHA256 hash)\n            value: Intent mapping to cache (list[IntentClassification])\n        \"\"\"\n        # Prune cache if at capacity (evict oldest entry - first in dict)\n        if len(self._cache) >= self.max_size:\n            oldest_key = next(iter(self._cache))\n            del self._cache[oldest_key]\n            logger.debug(f\"Cache pruned oldest entry (size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n\n        # Store with expiry timestamp\n        expiry_timestamp = time.time() + self.ttl_seconds\n        self._cache[key] = (value, expiry_timestamp)\n        logger.debug(f\"Cache STORED key {key[:8]} (cache size: {len(self._cache)})\")\n\n    def _cleanup_expired(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Remove all expired entries from the cache.\n\n        Returns:\n            Number of entries removed\n        \"\"\"\n        current_time = time.time()\n        expired_keys = [\n            key for key, (_, expiry) in self._cache.items()\n            if expiry < current_time\n        ]\n\n        for key in expired_keys:\n            del self._cache[key]\n\n        if expired_keys:\n            logger.debug(f\"Cache cleanup removed {len(expired_keys)} expired entries\")\n\n        return len(expired_keys)\n\n    def get_stats(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get cache statistics.\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict with hits, misses, hit_rate, size\n        \"\"\"\n        total_requests = self._cache_hits + self._cache_misses\n        hit_rate = (self._cache_hits / total_requests * 100) if total_requests > 0 else 0.0\n\n        return {\n            \"hits\": self._cache_hits,\n            \"misses\": self._cache_misses,\n            \"hit_rate\": hit_rate,\n            \"size\": len(self._cache),\n        }\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear the cache and reset statistics.\"\"\"\n        self._cache.clear()\n        self._cache_hits = 0\n        self._cache_misses = 0\n        logger.debug(\"Cache cleared\")\n\n    def should_log_stats(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if stats should be logged based on operation interval.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if stats should be logged, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        total_requests = self._cache_hits + self._cache_misses\n        return total_requests > 0 and total_requests % self._stats_log_interval == 0\n\n\n# Router error types for degraded-state handling\nclass RouterError(Exception):\n {parse_error}\")\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP 32-character hex string\n            >>> generate_cache_key(\"check pods\", None)  # Same as no context\n            'differenthashvalue...'  # Different hash without context\n        \"\"\"\n        import hashlib\n\n        # Handle None/empty context gracefully - treat as empty string\n        context = intent_type_context or \"\"\n\n        # Combine utterance and context with a delimiter to avoid collisions\n        # e.g., \"test\" + \"status\" vs \"teststatus\" + \"\"\n        combined = f\"{utterance}|{context}\"\n\n        # Generate SHA256 hash and return hex digest (32 characters)\n        return hashlib.sha256(combined.encode()).hexdigest()\n\n    def _get_cached_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\n        \"\"\"Check cache for existing classification.\"\"\"\n        cache_key = self.generate_cache_key(utterance, session_id)\n        return self._cache.get(cache_key)\n\n    def _cache_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache classification result.\"\"\"\n        cache_key = self.generate_cache_key(utterance, session_id)\n        self._cache.set(cache_key, classifications)\n\n    def _get_cache_stats_string(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get formatted cache statistics string for logging.\n\n        Returns:\n            Formatted string with cache hit rate and entries count\n        \"\"\"\n        stats = self._cache.get_stats()\n        return f\"cache_hit_rate={stats['hit_rate']:.2f}% entries={stats['size']}\"\n\n    def _log_cache_stats_if_needed(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Log cache hit rate statistics periodically.\"\"\"\n        if self._cache.should_log_stats():\n            stats = self._cache.get_stats()\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Router cache statistics: \"\n                f\"hits={stats['hits']} misses={stats['misses']} \"\n                f\"hit_rate={stats['hit_rate']:.1f}% \"\n                f\"total_requests={stats['hits'] + stats['misses']} \"\n                f\"cache_size={stats['size']}\"\n            )\n\n    def _clear_cache(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear the router cache. Used primarily for testing and diagnostics.\"\"\"\n        self._cache.clear()\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n\n        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n        when the file mtime changes. Log cache statistics periodically\n            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n\n            # Log cache hit with cache statistics in timing breakdown format\n            cache_stats = self._get_cache_stats_string()\n            logger.info(\n                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n                f\"cached=True \"\n                f\"intents={len(cached_result)} \"\n                f\"{cache_stats}\"\n            )\n\n            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n            empty_breakdown = {\n                \"cached\": True,\n                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n                \"process_ms\": 0,\n                \"total_ms\": 0,\n                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n            }\n            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n\n        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n        retry_count = 0\n        raw_response = None\n\n        try:\n            # Measure total classification time\n            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n\n            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n\n            # Measure prompt construction time (template rendering, message formatting)\n            # This isolates client-side preparation cost from network and inference time\n            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n\n            # Build user message\n            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=96,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 128 to 96 - intent JSON is typically 40-80 tokens (single: ~40-60, multi: ~80-100)\n                temperature=0.0,\n                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n            )\n            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n\n            # Extract content and timing from response\n            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n\n            # Store raw response for error reporting\n            raw_response = response\n\n            # Calculate model inference time: proxy response time - network time\n            # This gives us the actual model inference duration by subtracting network RTT\n            calculated_inference_ms = None\n            if timing_network_ms is not None:\n                calculated_inference_ms = proxy_ms - timing_network_ms\n\n            # Measure JSON parsing time\n            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n            try:\n                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n            except ParseLLMError as e:\n                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n                raise e\n            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure classification processing time\n            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n\n            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n\n            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n            calculated_inference_str = f\"{calculated_inference_ms:.2f}\" if calculated_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n            cache_stats = self._get_cache_stats_string()\n            logger.info(\n                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n                f\"proxy_inference_ms={calculated_inference_str} \"\n                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"intents={len(classifications)} \"\n                f\"{cache_stats}\"\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n\n            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n            timing_breakdown = {\n                \"cached\": False,  # Cache miss - result from fresh ZAI proxy call\n                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(calculated_inference_ms, 2) if calculated_inference_ms is not None else None,\n                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n            }\n\n            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n\n            # Log cache statistics periodically (including this cache miss)\n            self._log_cache_stats_if_needed()\n\n            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n\n        except ParseLLMError as e:\n            \"\"\"\n            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n\n            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n            \"\"\"\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n\n            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n                retry_count += 1\n                try:\n                    # Retry once with same parameters\n                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n                        utterance=utterance,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n                    )\n                    return classifications, _\n                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n                        retry_count=retry_count,\n                    ) from retry_e\n\n            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n            raise RouterMalformedError(\n                parse_error=str(e),\n                raw_output=raw_output,\n                retry_count=retry_count,\n            ) from e\n\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        try:\n            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n            try:\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n\n            routed_intents = []\n            for classification in classifications:\n                # Create intent ID\n                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                    intent_id=intent_id,\n                    classification=classification,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                    router_ms=router_ms,\n                )\n                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n            return routed_intents\n\n        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterProxyError as e:\n            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n            }\n\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            }\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n                classification.intent_type,\n                classification.lookup_kind\n            )\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n\n            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n                # Increment completed count\n                completed_sources[0] += 1\n\n                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n                    total=total_sources,\n                    source_name=source.value,\n                    source_status=result.status,\n                ))\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n                )\n\n                # Build failed sources list for error event\n                failed_sources = []\n                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n                    failed_sources.append({\n                        \"source\": source.value,\n                        \"status\": result.status,\n                        \"error\": result.error,\n                    })\n\n                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                }\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            try:\n                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            except Exception as synth_e:\n                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n                )\n\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_degraded_raw_data(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    f\"Synthesize failed: {synth_e}\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n                    \"degraded\": True,\n                }\n\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification (includes lookup_kind for lookups)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n                \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                topic_type=\"exception\",\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n            topic_id = None\n\n        # Handle terminal failure (updates intent
