topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType, lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=\"stuck\",\n                status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            #          data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=routed_intent.classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if )\n180\t\n181\t            request_end_ms = time.perf_counter() * 1000\n182\t\n183\t            # Check for rate limit\n184\t            if response.status_code == 429:\n185\t                raise request_start_ms\n207\t\n208\t            # Improved network latency estimation\n209\t            # For small responses (router), network is typically 100-200ms on VPN\n210\t            # For larger responses, proportionally more time on transfer\n211\t            # Base latency + transfer time based on output tokens\n212\t            base_network_latency = 100  # Base VPN round-trip latency\n213\t            if output_tokens > 0:\n214\t                # Transfer time: ~0.3ms per token on typical connection\n215\t                transfer_time = output_tokens * 0.3\n216\t                estimated_network_ms = base_network_latency + transfer_time\n217\t            else:\n218\t                estimated_network_ms = base_network_latency\n219\t\n220\t            # Cap network max_network_ms)\n223\t\n224\t            # Inference time is total minus network overhead\n225\t            estimated_inference_ms = max(0, total_ms - estimated_network_ms)\n226\t\n227\t       except Exception as e:\n255\t            raise LLMError(f\"LLM request failed: {e}\") from e\n256\t\n257\t    async def call_simple(\n258\t        self,\n259\t        system_prompt: str,\n260\t user_message: User message\n272\t            model: Model to use (defaults to client's default_model)\n273\t            max_tokens: Maximum tokens to generate\n274\t            temperature: Sampling \"https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages\",\n)\n\n# Default model for bead formulation\nDEFAULT_MODEL = \"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\"\n\n\nclass ModelClass(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Available model classes.\n\n    Performance note: For routing tasks, SONNET is empirically faster than HAIKU\n    (median ~2362ms vs ~3861ms in our tests). Use SONNET for router despite HAIKU\n    being marketed as \"fast\". SONNET provides better price-performance for this use case.\n    \"\"\"\n    HAIKU = \"claude-haiku-4-20250514\"  # Fast, cheap - but slower for routing in practice\n    SONNET = \"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\"  # Balanced - fastest for routing tasks\n    OPUS = \"claude-opus-4-20250514\"  # Highest quality - for complex tasks\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass LLMRequest:\n    \"\"\"A request to the LLM.\"\"\"\n    system_prompt: str\n    user_message: str\n    model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL\n    max_tokens: int = 4096\n    temperature: float = 0.7\n\n    def to_payload(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Convert to API payload.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"model\": self.model,\n            \"max_tokens\": self.max_tokens,\n            \"temperature\": self.temperature,\n            \"system\": self.system_prompt,\n            \"messages\": [\n                {\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": self.user_message}\n            ],\n        }\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass LLMResponse:\n    \"\"\"Response from the LLM.\"\"\"\n    content: str\n    model: str\n    input_tokens: int\n    output_tokens: int\n    finish_reason: str | None = None\n    timing_network_ms: float | None = None  # Network latency (first byte received)\n    timing_total_ms: float | None = None  # Total round-trip time\n\n    @property\n    def total_tokens(self) -> int:\n        return self.input_tokens + self.output_tokens\n\n    @property\n    def timing_inference_ms(self) -> float | None:\n        \"\"\"Estimated model inference time (total - network).\"\"\"\n        if self.timing_total_ms is not None and self.timing_network_ms is not None:\n            return max(0, self.timing_total_ms - self.timing_network_ms)\n        return       default_model: str = DEFAULT_MODEL,\n        timeout: float = 30.0,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.proxy_url = proxy_url\n        self.default_model = default_model\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self._client: Optional[httpx.AsyncClient] = None\n\n    async def _get_client(self) -> httpx.AsyncClient:\n        \"\"\"Get or create HTTP client with connection pooling.\"\"\"\n        if self._client is None:\n            # Configure connection pooling for reduced latency\n            # - HTTP/1.1 keepalive for connection reuse\n            # - Connection limits for ZAI proxy\n            # - Softer timeouts for connection establishment\n            limits = httpx.Limits(\n                max_keepalive_connections=10,\n                max_connections=20,\n                keepalive_expiry=30.0\n            )\n            self._client = httpx.AsyncClient(\n                timeout=self.timeout,\n                verify=False,\n                limits=limits,\n      \"\"\"Close the HTTP client.\"\"\"\n        if self._client:\n            await self._client.aclose()\n            self._client = None\n\n    async def call(self, request: LLMRequest) -> LLMResponse:\n        \"\"\"\n        Make a single-turn LLM call.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The LLM request\n\n        Returns:\n            LLMResponse with content, usage info, and timing breakdown\n\n        Raises:\n            LLMTimeoutError: If request times out\n            LLMRateLimitError: If rate limited\n            LLMError: For other errors\n        \"\"\"\n        import time\n        client = await self._get_client()\n\n        request_start_ms = time.perf_counter() * 1000\n\n        try:\n            payload = request.to_payload()\n            logger.debug(f\"LLM request: model={request.model}, input_tokens_estimate={len(request.user_message) // 4}\")\n\n            response = await client.post(\n                self.proxy_url,\n                json=payload,\n                headers={\n                    \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n                },\n            )\n\n            request_end_ms = time.perf_counter() * 1000\n\n            # Check for rate limit\n            if response.status_code == 429:\n                raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\")\n\n            response.raise_for_status()\n            data = response.json()\n\n            # ZAI proxy wraps the Anthropic response under \"result\"\n            payload_inner = data.get(\"result\", data)\n\n            # Extract content from response\n            content = payload_inner.get(\"content\", [])\n            if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n                text = content[0].get(\"text\", \"\")\n            else:\n                text = str(content)\n\n            # Extract usage\n            usage = payload_inner.get(\"usage\", data.get(\"usage\", {}))\n            input_tokens = usage.get(\"input_tokens\", 0)\n            output_tokens = usage.get(\"output_tokens\", 0)\n\n            # Calculate timing breakdown\n            total_ms = request_end_ms - request_start_ms\n\n            # Improved network latency estimation\n            # For small responses (router), network is typically 100-200ms on VPN\n            # For larger responses, proportionally more time on transfer\n            # Base latency + transfer time based on output tokens\n            base_network_latency = 100  # Base VPN round-trip latency\n            if output_tokens > 0:\n                # Transfer time: ~0.3ms per token on typical connection\n                transfer_time = output_tokens * 0.3\n                estimated_network_ms = base_network_latency + transfer_time\n            else:\n                estimated_network_ms = base_network_latency\n\n            # Cap network estimate at 40% of total time (inference must take some time)\n            max_network_ms = total_ms * 0.4\n            estimated_network_ms = min(estimated_network_ms, max_network_ms)\n\n            # Inference time is total minus network overhead\n            estimated_inference_ms = max(0, total_ms - estimated_network_ms)\n\n            logger.debug(\n                f\"LLM response: input_tokens={input_tokens}, output_tokens={output_tokens}, request failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def call_simple(\n        self,\n        system_prompt: str,\n        user_message: str,\n        model: str | None = None,\n        max_tokens: int = 4096,\n        temperature: float = 0.7,\n        return_timing: bool = False,\n    ) -> str | dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Convenience method for simple LLM calls.\n\n        Args:\n = 4096,\n        temperature: float = 0.7,\n    ) -> AsyncGenerator[Union[str, Dict[str, Any]], None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Stream LLM response for progressive card fill.\n\n        Yields text chunks as they arrive from the API.\n\n        Args:\n            system_prompt: System prompt for the LLM\n            user_message: User message\n            model: Model to use (defaults to client's default_model)\n            max_tokens: Maximum tokens to generate\n            temperature: Sampling temperature\n\n        Yields:\n            Text chunks as they arrive\n\n        Returns:\n            Complete response text and usage info in the final iteration\n        \"\"\"\n        request = LLMRequest(\n            system_prompt=system_prompt,\n            user_message=user_message,\n            model=model or self.default_model,\n            max_tokens=max_tokens,\n            temperature=temperature,\n        )\n\n        client = await self._get_client()\n\n        try:\n            payload = request.to_payload()\n            payload[\"stream\"] = True  # Enable streaming\n\n            logger.debug(f\"LLM streaming request: model={request.model}\")\n\n            async with client.stream(\n                \"POST\",\n                self.proxy_url,\n                json=payload,\n                headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"},\n            ) as response:\n                # Check for rate limit\n                if response.status_code == 429:\n                    raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\")\n\n                response.raise_for_status()\n\n                accumulated_text = []\n                input_tokens = 0\n                output_tokens = 0\n\n                #  accumulated_text.append(text)\n                                    yield text\n\n                            # Extract final usage\n                            if data.get(\"type\") == \"message_stop\":\n                                usage = data.get(\"usage\", {})\n                                input_tokens = usage.get(\"input_tokens\", 0)\n                                output_tokens = usage.get(\"output_tokens\", 0)\n\n                        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                            logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse streaming event: {e}\")\n\n                # Yield final result with usage info\n                full_text = \"\".join(accumulated_text)\n                logger.debug(f\"LLM streaming complete: input_tokens={input_tokens}, output_tokens={output_tokens}\")\n                yield {\n                    \"text\": full_text,\n                    \"input_tokens\": input_tokens,\n                    \"output_tokens\": output_tokens,\n                    \"finish_reason\": \"stop\",\n                }\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError as e:\n            raise LLMTimeoutError(f\"LLM streaming request timed out after {self.timeout}s\") from e\n        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:\n            raise LLMTimeoutError(f\"LLM streaming request timed out: {e}\") from e\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 429:\n                raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\") from e\n            raise LLMError(f\"LLM streaming request failed: {e.response.status_code}\") from e\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, LLMRateLimitError, LLMError):\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            raise LLMError(f\"LLM streaming request failed: {e}\") from e\n\n\n# Global ZAI client instance\n_client: Optional[ZAIClient] = None\n\n\ndef get_zai_client(\n    proxy_url: str = ZAI_PROXY_URL,\n    default_model: str =
